w:r.w:t: I hereby declare that the work presented in this project titled
is an original and authentic piece of work carried out by me in partial fulfillment of the requirements for the award of the degree of Master of Computer Applications (MCA) at
This project has been completed under the guidance of
, and has not been submitted to any other university or institution for the award of any degree or diploma.
, has successfully completed the project work entitled
“DataPilotAI – A One AI Powered Document Intelligence Platform”
out in partial fulfillment of the requirements for the award of the degree of
. To the best of my knowledge, the work embodies the student’s own
part or in full, for the award of any
degree or diploma in any other institution or university.
shown sincerity, dedication, and professionalism throughout the project duration.
This project report presents the development contributions made to
reliable document intelligence professionals. As a

, I developed clean and intuitive user interfaces that ensured a smooth user experience across different devices and screen sizes. The responsive behavior of the platform was achieved through modern CSS techniques and frameworks to enhance accessibility and usability. I also implemented robust
to improve data accuracy and user feedback during form interactions.

Additionally, I integrated multiple RESTful APIs to enable dynamic interactions between the frontend and backend, particularly in areas such as user authentication, document upload, and data display. The project provided real-world exposure to agile development, collaborative coding, and quality testing processes.
This report outlines my practical contributions in building a production-ready web interface and demonstrates how front-end engineering plays a vital
role in the success of modern AI document platforms like DataPilotAI.
, Project Supervisor, for his consistent guidance, expert feedback, and constructive support throughout the duration of this project
Their valuable insights greatly contributed to the clarity and direction of this report.
academic support and for providing a structured environment that facilitated the smooth execution of this project.
I would like to express my gratitude to the management of
for offering me the opportunity to work on a live project as part of their development team. I especially acknowledge the contributions of the
knowledge in the areas of UI design, responsive development, form validation, and API
This acknowledgement reflects my appreciation for the professional and academic support received during the course of this project.

DataPilotAI is a comprehensive web-based platform developed using the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js). It serves as an AI-powered document intelligence platform, enabling users to seamlessly perform a range of tasks such as PDF text extraction, RAG-based analysis, semantic
platform is designed to process documents through an intelligent AI pipeline, ensuring convenience, reliability, and quality service delivery through a secure and scalable full-stack architecture.
This report outlines the practical development experience gained during the final semester internship at
, where the role was designated as

. The contribution primarily focused on designing and implementing the front-end components of the platform, ensuring mobile responsiveness, validating user inputs, and integrating the user interface with
backend services through RESTful APIs. Additionally, efforts were made to test key functionalities and ensure a smooth user experience across devices and platforms.

The primary objective of this project is to provide hands-on exposure to real-world full-stack web development using modern technologies. By actively contributing to the development of DataPilotAI, the project offers a practical learning experience in designing and building a scalable document intelligence application.
Collaborating in a real-time deployment environment and understanding code versioning, testing, and debugging workflows.
The scope of the project includes a defined set of development activities focused on front-end functionality and its interaction with backend services. Specific areas covered are:
Developing device-friendly, interactive, and modern interfaces using React.js to enhance user experience across different screen
Implementing secure and efficient input validation mechanisms using libraries such as
to ensure data integrity and enhance security.
in handling and managing user and AI agent data within the application.
Gaining familiarity with working in real project deployment setups, including development, testing, and staging environments.

Solutions is an innovative software development company dedicated to delivering high-quality digital solutions for web and mobile platforms. The company primarily focuses on building scalable, efficient, and user-centric applications using the MERN (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js) technology
a client-first approach and a strong emphasis on modern development practices,
Solutions supports businesses across various domains in creating impactful digital
The organization maintains a collaborative development environment, encouraging continuous learning and innovation among its teams. By leveraging cutting-edge technologies,
Solutions aims to solve real-world problems through custom software solutions tailored to client needs.
DataPilotAI is one of the key products developed by

Solutions. It is a comprehensive platform that allows users to easily find, upload, and review local AI models, for intelligent document processing and AI-powered analysis. The application is built using the MERN stack — React.js for the frontend, Node.js and Express.js for the backend, and MongoDB for the database.

features such as real-time document processing, seamless document upload and AI analysis, multi-LLM support, and vector database integration. DataPilotAI bridges the gap between users and their document data, making document analysis more accessible, transparent, and efficient.
by leveraging technology and user-first design.
Solutions operates through a well-structured team-based system, with each department playing a crucial role in the development and maintenance of projects.
Responsible for creating the client-facing part of web applications using technologies such as React.js, HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript.
Handles server-side logic and database management using
data flow, API creation, security, and scalability.
Focuses on the look, feel, and usability of the product. The team designs wireframes, prototypes, and final layouts to enhance the overall user experience.
Ensures the reliability and performance of
applications through rigorous testing methods, including manual and automated testing,
bug tracking, and quality assurance processes.
Manages deployment pipelines, server configurations, and cloud services. This team ensures that applications are securely deployed, monitored, and maintained in production environments.

In the traditional approach to manual document intelligence, users often depend on informal recommendations, manual document review processes, or unverified local listings to find professionals like basic PDF readers and manual text search tools. This method typically lacks consistency in service quality, analysis accuracy, and trustworthiness. Many users struggle with identifying reliable professionals, managing document queries, and ensuring accountability after the service is delivered.
Furthermore, without a proper technological platform, the process remains manual
and inefficient — involving direct phone calls, uncertain
availability, and no standard means of

payment or performance tracking. This fragmented experience has created a gap in the market for a digital solution that can connect users with verified AI agents while ensuring convenience, reliability, and transparency.
Users do not have access to a unified application that offers a wide range of verified AI models in one
often requires multiple phone calls and follow-ups, leading to delays and
Transparency in Pricing and Service Quality:
There is no standard pricing system or method for evaluating a AI agent’s performance before hiring them.
Absence of Real-time Tracking and Feedback Mechanism:
Users are unable to track AI models or share their service experience through structured reviews, which affects future decision-making for other users.
DataPilotAI addresses the above challenges by offering a digital solution that enhances the document intelligence experience for users and professionals alike. The key features and benefits include:
DataPilotAI brings together a wide range of document intelligence categories into a single, easy-to-use platform. Users can upload, search, and manage documents directly from the app.
The platform ensures quality and safety by on boarding only verified and background-checked AI models. This builds trust and reduces the risk of poor service.
time, and expertise. This reduces delays and increases efficiency.
Integrated Payment Gateway and Review System:
Users can make secure payments online and leave ratings or reviews after service completion. This
not only improves transparency but also helps maintain a high standard of service quality.
DataPilotAI effectively transforms the fragmented, offline document intelligence industry into a tech-driven, user-friendly ecosystem that prioritizes trust, convenience, and

w:r.w:t: The primary objectives of the DataPilotAI platform development project were aligned with building a responsive, efficient, and scalable web application that connects users with various AI document analysiss. The project aimed to improve the overall digital service delivery experience by offering a seamless interface for both customers and AI agents. The main goals of the project were as follows:
To develop and test responsive UI pages for both user and AI agent dashboards

: The UI design was aimed at providing a smooth and intuitive experience on different devices, including desktops, tablets, and mobile phones. A mobile-first approach was adopted using vector database integration frameworks like
components, to ensure consistency across various screen sizes and browsers.
To implement robust form validation and error-handling mechanisms
: Forms played a vital role in functionalities such as user registration, login, document upload, and feedback collection. Implementing proper validation
rules ensured that user inputs adhered to required formats, which reduced invalid data submissions and enhanced user experience. Client-side validation was done using
, while meaningful error messages were provided to guide users in correcting input mistakes.
: A key goal was to enable real-time interaction between the frontend and backend services

. This included operations like fetching available services, authenticating users, processing documents, and collecting user feedback. APIs ensured dynamic data flow and allowed the application to be more scalable and maintainable.
To assist in application deployment and bug fixing
: Supporting the deployment process was crucial to ensure that the developed application transitioned smoothly from
members to resolve functionality issues before release.
working on the DataPilotAI platform under the guidance of the development team at
, my responsibilities were primarily focused on the frontend development, UI/UX design, form handling, and integration of APIs. I actively participated in the development cycle and contributed
Designed and tested the document upload UI using React.js and Tailwind CSS
: I was responsible for building the document upload interface from scratch using
, a popular JavaScript library for building user interfaces. I structured the component hierarchy, managed state effectively using React hooks, and applied
classes to ensure the layout was responsive. I also performed rigorous testing to verify
: To enhance form management and input validation, I used
to handle form states and events, while
was used to define schema-based validation rules. The validation
as login, signup, document upload, and feedback forms. This approach not only saved development time but also maintained consistency across the application.
Integrated REST APIs for login, document upload, and user feedback

for making asynchronous HTTP requests. For the login feature, I ensured secure form submission and handled success/error responses appropriately. For document upload and feedback, I connected frontend components to backend endpoints, allowing users to interact with
and response messages were also managed to improve user interaction.
Participated in daily standups and bug fixing
collaborated with the development team through
During the testing and stabilization phases, I was involved in identifying UI issues, resolving cross-browser compatibility
problems, and addressing layout glitches. My contributions ensured that the final product met both functional and visual expectations.
Working on the DataPilotAI platform as part of my final semester project provided me with valuable hands-on experience in a real-world software development
environment. It helped bridge the gap between academic knowledge and industry practices. The key learning outcomes from this project include:
Exposure to real-world MERN development cycle
: I gained practical experience in working with the
technology stack. While my focus was on the frontend part
(React.js), understanding the entire development pipeline helped me
appreciate how different components interact in a full-stack architecture.
Improved understanding of API logic and frontend-backend communication
: Through API integration tasks, I developed a clear understanding of
methods (GET, POST), error handling, and the importance of status codes. I also learned to manage asynchronous data using
, ensuring smooth data flow between frontend components and backend services.

: I frequently encountered bugs during development and learned how to debug JavaScript and React code effectively using browser dev tools, React Developer Tools, and console logging. This improved my problem-solving approach and enabled me to deliver cleaner, bug-free code.
Better code management using GitHub and version control

for version control, including creating branches, committing code, handling pull requests, resolving merge conflicts, and collaborating with team members. This experience significantly improved my ability to work in a team and maintain a clean and traceable codebase.
Enhanced communication and team collaboration skills
updating progress regularly helped me build confidence and improve my communication skills, both verbal and written, which are essential in a professional work environment.
the requirements, structure, and feasibility of the application to ensure it meets the intended purpose. For the DataPilotAI platform, the analysis included understanding the needs of
various stakeholders (users, AI agents, and administrators), identifying system functionalities, and evaluating both technical and operational feasibility.

Requirement analysis involved gathering and interpreting the specific functionalities that the DataPilotAI platform should deliver. Based on the scope and objective of the platform, the requirements were categorized for two key stakeholders: Users and
Users should be able to create accounts, log in securely, and manage their profiles.
specific services (e.g., semantic search, PDF text extraction).
Option to delete previously uploaded documents with appropriate
submit reviews for service quality assessment.
and accept or reject them based on availability.
The system tracks document status as pending, processing, completed, or failed.
manage completed service logs for accounting.
This bifurcation of workflows ensures that the platform provides role-specific access and features.
DataPilotAI platform, the following were identified as key functional components:
rating, or availability are provided to narrow down service choices.
Payment integration (via mock or third-party API) ensures transaction completion and record-keeping.
After completing a service, users can provide star ratings and written
users, uploads, AI agent activity, and traffic
suspension, category management, and report generation.
what it does. These are essential to ensure user satisfaction, maintainability, and scalability.
to ensure mobile responsiveness and cross-platform support.
requests efficiently, with minimal response latency.
User credentials and sensitive data are stored securely using password hashing (
different perspectives to determine whether it can be successfully developed and maintained.
simplifying code management and deployment workflows.
significantly reduced licensing and infrastructure costs.
system cost-effective for prototyping and deployment.
to operate by users, AI agents, and admins.
critical functions are accessible through intuitive UI components.
handling document queries were streamlined through clear workflows.

The DataPilotAI application is structured in a modular and component-driven architecture. Each layer of the system is responsible for a specific role, ensuring separation of concerns and maintainability.
Branching strategies and pull requests were used for version control.
This chapter explores previous research, existing applications, and technological studies

document intelligence systems and the MERN (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js) technology stack. The review was conducted by analyzing scholarly journals, open-source projects, product case studies, and official documentation. The goal was to identify best practices, design patterns, common pitfalls, and relevant frameworks to guide the development of DataPilotAI.
Several well-established platforms were examined to understand user needs and technical implementation strategies:
document intelligence such as PDF question answering, semantic search, and text extraction. Their platform uses microservices and ensures high availability and scalability.
Provides AI-powered document analysis, with a strong emphasis on quality control and AI agent management. Their UX focuses on simplicity and trust-
Targets smaller cities and emphasizes convenience in document uploads with a simplified mobile-first interface.
access, intuitive dashboards, feedback mechanisms, and secure payment tracking. A common theme across all platforms was the need for
layout across devices of varying screen sizes.
to determine the most efficient way to manage asynchronous API calls.
security loopholes like injection attacks and data tampering.
architecture and applying both frontend and backend best practices.
DataPilotAI is an AI-powered document intelligence platform built using the MERN stack. The system integrates
for scalable document processing and semantic retrieval.
Optimized layout and components using React and Tailwind CSS to support all device types.
frontend and backend using well-defined routes.
Role-specific views for users, AI agents, and admins to manage activities and monitor performance.
Signup/login using JWT-based authentication, with validations on every input field.
From the dashboard, the user selects a category (e.g., PDF Upload, Semantic Search).
The admin panel reflects the document upload and allows assignment tracking and status updates.
presents the overall system from a high-level perspective, showcasing the data interaction
breaks down key functions such as user registration, document upload, and feedback into sub-processes. These diagrams will be included in the Annexure for visual reference.
System design translates requirements into structured models. For DataPilotAI, this involved
collections This design promotes separation of concerns and scalability.
Core web technologies for markup, styling, and
reducing manual effort for users and increasing efficiency in document processing.
